When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

- Right token (8) is adjacent to gap bridge (7) and purple wall (2)

The purple wall (2) is adjacent to the right platform (3) and the right token (8).

Then DOWN to reach the container:

- DOWN → [27,39]-[29,41]

So the plan for the right token is:

- RIGHT x4: [18,45]-[20,47]
- DOWN x3: [27,45]-[29,47]
That's 11 moves. But first I need to undo the LEFT to get back to original position [30,27]-[32,29]. Actually, the token is at [30,30]-[32,32], which is 3 cols right of the original [30,27]-[32,29]. So I need LEFT first.
Plan:
1. LEFT (back to [30,27]-[32,29])
2. UP x4 (to [18,27]-[20,29])
3. RIGHT x4 (to [18,42]-[20,44]... wait, let me recalculate)
From [30,27]-[32,29]:
